63.reason: [Passing 2D array c as char* to bubble is type-incompatible; also, N=500 doesn't match actual row size of c which is 500 bytes but declared as c[2000][500]]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [Passing 2D array c as char* to bubble is type-incompatible; also, N=500 doesn't match actual row size of c which is 500 bytes but declared as c[2000][500]]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Passing 2D array c as char* to bubble is type-incompatible; also, N=500 doesn't match actual row width of c (500 vs declared 500 columns but used as 500-byte strings).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [The bubble function expects strings of max length N=500, but c is declared as c[2000][500], so each string is 500 chars. However, words were copied without null terminators, so strcmp in bubble will read beyond intended word boundaries, causing undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [The third argument '500' is used as the string width in bubble sort, but the actual string width in array 'c' is 500 (second dimension), which matches. However, the logic in bubble assumes fixed-width strings, but words may be shorter, leading to comparison of garbage beyond null terminators. Also, if s==0, passing c (which may be uninitialized) could cause issues.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [The third argument to bubble should match the actual string length allocated per entry in c, which is 500, but c is declared as c[2000][500], so passing 500 is correct. However, the logic in bubble assumes fixed-width strings, which may not align with how words are stored (not null-padded to full width), leading to incorrect comparisons or overreads.]

68.reason: [b1[0] should be initialized before incrementing; logic error in counting]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[0] should be initialized before incrementing; logic error in counting]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[0] should be initialized before incrementing; logic error in counting duplicates.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [b1[0] is set to 1, but if s==0, this writes to b1[0] unnecessarily. More critically, b1 array is not initialized, so if q>0 and we hit the 'else' branch, b1[q-1]++ operates on uninitialized value.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[0] is set to 1, but if s==0 this loop doesn't run; however, more critically, b1[q] is used without initialization for q>0 in other branches. Also, b1[q]++ below uses uninitialized b1[q].]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[0] is set to 1, but if multiple identical words follow, b1[q] is incremented without initializing b1[q] first in the 'else' branch, leading to use of uninitialized values.]

73.reason: [b1[q] used uninitialized; should be b1[q] = 1 or similar]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[q] was never initialized to 1 before incrementing; leads to undefined behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [b1[q] is used before being initialized. It should be set to 1 first, not incremented. Also, d[q] is overwritten but previous d[q] might be garbage. Logic for counting is flawed.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[q] is used uninitialized here. It should be set to 1 first, not incremented.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [b1[q] is used without being initialized to 1 first. This leads to undefined behavior due to reading uninitialized memory.]

77.reason: [This increments previous count even when current word is different, leading to incorrect counts]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This increments previous count even when current word is different, leading to incorrect counts]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This increments previous count even when current word is new, causing incorrect counts.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This increments count for previous word when a new word is found, but it should have been counted already during its run. This leads to overcounting. Also, b1[q-1] may be uninitialized.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [This increments the count for the previous word, but it should have been initialized when that word was first encountered. The counting logic is flawed: it only increments when a new different word is found, missing the last group.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.2 , reason:  [This increments the count for the previous word, but if the previous word was already finalized, this double-counts or miscounts. Also, new distinct words don't get b1 initialized properly.]

50.reason: [This increments s after copying a word, but the copied word may be empty if j==p (e.g., consecutive non-letter chars). Also, the null terminator is not added to c[s], leading to undefined behavior in strcmp later.]
